items.title: Tesla misses on earnings, as free cash flow turns negative and margins slide
items.description: Tesla's earnings report lands in the midst of a slide in the company's stock price even as its core auto business is rebounding.

items.title: U.S. signs nuclear power agreement with Saudi Arabia
items.description: U.S. cooperation with foreign countries on civilian nuclear power requires review by Congress under the Atomic Energy Act.

items.title: Reddit stock sinks on report it may not renew Google AI content deal
items.description: Google's AI summaries have reduced traffic to websites from the search results page and Reddit is reconsidering the benefits of the deal, the Journal said.

items.title: Senate crypto bill would ban federal officials — including presidents — from issuing digital assets
items.description: A new version of the Clarity Act would impose the first limits on federal officials sponsoring or issuing cryptocurrency.

items.title: Southwest Airlines profit jumps 9% as passengers pick up fuel tab, but third-quarter forecast falls short
items.description: Southwest Airlines reported a more than 9% increase in second-quarter profit as higher fares are increasingly helping the airline cover its fuel tab.

items.title: Ex-Google recruiter: Ask this 'insightful' question in an interview and I'll want to hire you 'on the spot'
items.description: Former Google recruiter Farah Sharghi reveals the question that makes job candidates stand out to her the most.

items.title: IBM lowers full-year forecast after earnings warning
items.description: IBM is now looking to improve productivity with artificial intelligence, including with a new coding tool called Bob.

items.title: U.S. Secret Service official: 'Threat environment is the highest we've ever seen'
items.description: The Secret Service has seen a 40% increase this year in cases involving threats to protectees, including President Donald Trump.

items.title: Amazon cuts some jobs in its artificial general intelligence unit
items.description: Amazon's AGI unit has released a set of AI models, called Nova, and also includes groups working on silicon and quantum computing.

items.title: Analysis: Iran war energy shock hits the U.S. economy as gas and diesel prices climb
items.description: A new asymmetry in the market means fuel prices won't fall quickly even if the shooting in the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz stops.

items.title: Senate panel advances China auto bill that could bar Mercedes-Benz from U.S.
items.description: Mercedes-Benz’s largest individual shareholder is Chinese state-owned BAIC. Cruz warned the bill could hurt Mercedes and accused GM of backing it.

items.title: EU antitrust regulators clear Paramount-WBD merger as it faces challenge by U.S. states
items.description: European regulators signed off on Paramount's proposed acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery.

items.title: J&J gets a big win on its new robotic surgical system — plus, a flood of earnings
items.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

items.title: Houthis deploy missiles and drones to attack ships in southern Red Sea, naval group says
items.description: Attacks on ships transiting the Bab el-Mandeb threaten to cut off millions of barrels per day of Saudi crude oil exports.

items.title: OpenAI cyber models broke out of training environment to hack Hugging Face
items.description: The incident is unique because it was "driven, end to end, by an autonomous AI agent system," according to Hugging Face.

items.title: Sen. Wyden introduces bill to curb Trump's tariff powers
items.description: Sen. Ron Wyden said Trump has "abused every trade authority at his disposal" and called his new tariffs on Canadian goods a "shakedown."

items.title: Samsung details features of its smart glasses as it enters market dominated by Meta
items.description: The Samsung smart glasses are the tech giant's first foray into the product category which is currently dominated by Meta.

items.title: Kevin Warsh has homed in on three key phrases. How Fed watchers interpret them
items.description: In five public appearances, Warsh has used the phrase "family fight" 13 times, returning to "first principles" 11 times and "inflation is a choice" for the Fed six times.

items.title: Congressional stock trading curb due to get House vote amid public support for a ban
items.description: The House is scheduled to vote on Wednesday on a bill that would bar members of Congress from purchasing additional stocks while in office.

items.title: Under-the-radar beverage name captures trader's attention
items.description: Michael Khouw breaks down how he's trading this stock ahead of earnings.
